*This observation rubric will be used for Q-comp observations conducted in accordance with Q-Comp. The results of the observation are not to be construed as a summative evaluation. The results of each observation are considered, though, in the summative evaluation process.

PERFORMANCE RUBRIC I Planning and Preparation

Performance Levels

Criteria Beginning Developing Applying Innovating

A. Demonstrates
appropriate knowledge
of content and
pedagogy
Evidence:		 Teacher displays minimal
knowledge of the content,
background knowledge or of
pedagogical issues.		 Teacher displays basic content
knowledge and some
awareness of background and
pedagogical knowledge, but
does not anticipate student
misconceptions.		 Teacher displays solid content
knowledge and makes connections
between the content and other
parts of the discipline. The
teacher’s pedagogical practices
reflect current research on best
practices within the discipline.		 Teacher displays extensive
content knowledge, with
evidence of continuing
pursuits of such knowledge.
Teacher implements a
repertoire of pedagogical
practices based on current
research.
B. Demonstrates
knowledge of Students		 Teacher demonstrates little or
no knowledge of students’
backgrounds, skills, abilities, or
interests; and doesn’t use this
information in planning.		 Teacher demonstrates partial
knowledge of students’
backgrounds, skills, interests,
and abilities and attempts to
use this in planning for the
class as a whole.		 Teacher demonstrates thorough
knowledge of students’
backgrounds, skills, interests, and
abilities and attempts to use this in
planning for the class as a whole
and for groups.		 Teacher demonstrates
extensive knowledge of
students’ backgrounds, skills,
interests, and abilities and
attempts to use this in
planning for individual
student learning.

F. Designing Student
Assessment
Evidence:		 Teacher’s approach to
assessing student learning
lacks alignment with the
instructional goals and contains
no clear criteria or standards
for student performance;
assessment results are not
used to design future
instruction and student
assessment.		 Teacher’s assessment plan is
partially aligned with the
instructional goals and
includes criteria and standards
for student performance that
are not clear or understood by
students; assessment results
are used to design future
instruction and checks for
understanding for the whole
class.		 Teacher’s assessment plan is
aligned with the instructional goals
and the criteria and standards for
student performance have been
clearly communicated and are
understood by students;
assessment results are used to
design future instruction and
checks for understanding for
groups of students and individuals.		 Teacher’s assessment plan is
aligned with the instructional
goals; criteria and standards
for student performance
have been developed with
student input and clearly
communicated; students
monitor own progress in
achieving goals; assessment
results are used to design
future work for individual
students.
G. Data-Driven Planning The teacher’s lesson plans
reflect little or no evidence that
the instructional planning
process uses data to plan for
meeting students’ needs.		 The teacher uses minimal data
in the instructional planning
process.		 Teacher uses data to optimize
resources in the planning process.		 The teacher uses data to plan
appropriate curricula,
implement instructional
strategies, and use resources
to promote learning for all
students.

PERFORMANCE AREA II

Instruction

Performance Levels

Criteria Beginning Developing Applying Innovating
A. Communicating with
Students
Evidence:		 Teacher’s oral and written
communication is unclear and/
or is inappropriate to students;
does not include learning
objectives or establish clear
expectations.		 Teacher’s oral and written
communication is somewhat
clear and makes an attempt to
connect the learning objective
with the instruction and
establishes clear expectations.		 Teacher’s oral and written
communication is clear and
appropriate to students. The
communication includes learning
objectives establishes clear
expectations.		 Teacher’s oral and written
communication is clearly
expressed; includes learning
objectives and clear
expectations, and indicates the
anticipation of possible student
misconceptions.
B. Using Questioning
and Discussion
Techniques		 Little use of higher-level
questioning and discussion
techniques to drive instruction
and guide student learning.
Minimal student participation.		 Some use of higher
questioning and discussion
techniques utilized to drive
instruction and guide student
learning, attempts to engage
students in discussion and
moderate student
participation.		 Teacher’s use of questioning and
discussion techniques reflect
higher level questioning, varied
ideas, and consistent
participation by most students.		 Teacher consistently uses
higher level question and
discussion techniques.
Teacher’s students formulate
many higher-level questions
and assume responsibility for
participation of all students in
discussions.

Performance Levels

Criteria Beginning Developing Applying Innovating
A. Reflecting on
Teaching
Evidence:		 Teacher lacks the ability to self-
reflect on instruction, student
work, and products. Teacher
struggles to bring solutions to

work and products. Teacher
can make appropriate
suggestions for improvement.		 Teacher’s reflection on
instruction, student work, and
products are accurate. Teacher
can cite general characteristics
and make specific suggestions for
improvement or alternative
strategies.		 Teacher’s reflection on
instruction, student work, and
products are highly accurate
and perceptive; teacher can
cite specific examples and draw
on extensive repertoire to
suggest alternative strategies.

G. Utilizing Technology Teacher incorporates little
technology in preparation or
delivery of instruction and
provides minimal access to
students as a resource; at
times, teacher utilizes email,
voice mail, or communicates in
other ways.		 Teacher occasionally
incorporates technology in
preparation or delivery of
instruction and provides
limited access to students as a
resource; teacher occasionally

communicates in other ways.		 Teacher incorporates technology
in preparation and delivery of
